title: atriplex hortensis subsp. desertorum
intro: Atriplex hortensis subsp. desertorum, also known as edible orach, is a subspecies of the Atriplex hortensis plant, which is a type of succulent plant belonging to the amaranth family. This plant is rich in various nutrients and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and gout. Edible orach has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health, likely due to its high levels of vitamins A and C, potassium, and other essential minerals. Edible orach can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, and is also edible, making it a popular ingredient in salads and other dishes.
